Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible nephrotic syndrome. HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. Lipid profile shows elevated total and LDL cholesterol. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management of possible acute or chronic kidney disease.
